commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - Owner-authored low-level process-runtime change with availability risk should stay in normal maintainer review; no narrow automated repair remains.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern was found; the diff adds local subprocess lifecycle code and tests without new dependencies, permissions, downloaded artifacts, or secret handling. (sha=3d9b1e67b52b9ff7c2921052c74838c151730302)
commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Kiro CLI execution through a new package-internal spawned process-group launcher, adds residual helper cleanup, tracks bounded pipe EOF, and expands Kiro/process-launcher regressions.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main launches Kiro through Process.run() and then attempts setpgid, leaving a source-visible pre-exec race and residual helper cleanup gap; I did not run live tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - Owner-authored low-level process-runtime change with availability risk should stay in normal maintainer review; no narrow automated repair remains.

target.title: fix: clean up Kiro CLI helper processes
target.body:
## Summary

- launch Kiro CLI commands with a dedicated process group established before `exec`
- terminate residual helpers after normal parent exit, escalating from `TERM` to `KILL` before draining inherited pipes
- continue group cleanup after the root exits, while preserving the original graceful-termination deadline
- identify escaped helpers by the exact stdout/stderr pipes they retain, even after `setsid` and reparenting
- snapshot and identity-check descendants before timeout/cancellation cleanup so helpers that create a new session cannot escape
- preserve child standard streams when the host starts with a standard descriptor closed
- preserve existing timeout, cancellation, output, and exit-status behavior

Follow-up to #1533 and #1535.

## Root Cause

The merged fix bounded pipe draining, but its best-effort `setpgid` call happened after `Foundation.Process.run()`. On macOS the child can cross `exec` before that call, causing `setpgid` to fail with `EACCES`. A normally exiting Kiro parent could therefore return while a pipe-holding helper survived outside any controllable process group.

The first repair established the group before `exec`, but a helper could still call `setsid`, retain stdout/stderr, and be reparented before the CLI parent exited. At that point group-only cleanup could not discover it. The final candidate records stable identities for the output pipes at launch and scans for holders only when a residual group exists or bounded drain does not reach EOF. Every signal is guarded by a process start-time identity check. Cleanup also keeps observing the original process group through the TERM grace period after the root exits, then escalates any remaining group members.

## Proof

Exact head: `3d9b1e67b52b9ff7c2921052c74838c151730302`

- focused Kiro tests: 32 passed
- focused process-launcher tests: 6 passed, including reparented same-group helpers that ignore TERM or finish cleanup during grace
- closed-stdout launcher regression: passed with the test runner's stdout descriptor unavailable
- `make test`: 470 deterministic selections across 40 shards passed
- `make check`: parser/package/release guards passed; SwiftFormat clean; SwiftLint 0 violations
- `git diff --check`: passed
- `swift build -c release --product CodexBarCLI`: passed
- release CLI normal-exit proof: parent exited normally while a helper created a new session, retained the output pipes, and ignored `HUP`/`TERM`; command returned parsed usage in 2.028s and removed the helper
- release CLI idle-termination proof: helper created a new session, retained the output pipes, and ignored `TERM`; command returned parsed usage in 10.904s and removed the helper
- release CLI SHA-256 for that runtime candidate: `b01cea7ccaee7fd2049f770eb321c0082026902934a1cb31094c88ebdb5fa69a`
- exact-diff local autoreview: no accepted or actionable findings
- Public Model Identifier Gate: PASS; the candidate adds no model identifiers, broad release-binary matches are unchanged repository/provider surface, and sanitized logs/public proof add none

The runtime proof used an isolated temporary home and synthetic account data. No live credentials, browser data, Keychain access, private payloads, or user-identifying paths were used.

## Risk

The new process launcher is package-internal and currently used only by Kiro. Pipe-holder discovery runs only for residual groups or failed EOF drain, and signals only exact pipe holders whose process start identity still matches. macOS process-group, escaped-session, grace-period, normal-exit, and closed-standard-descriptor behavior is covered locally; CI validates the conditional Linux implementation.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
